author: @PaddyNinja
text: No way Mr Smith, I was just watching a load of One Wheel XR videos and thinking I wonder if Mr Smith would go for something of a personal recreational vehicle and saying to myself "Nah he wouldn't do that, would he? As awesome as your science videos are, it's good to switch it up and do something completely unexpected.  Looking forward to see how this plays out sir.

replies.author: @jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: I think it's actually termed as slack,steep being roadbike,longer the fork travel the steeper the angle has to be in order for the fork to actually compress if it has suspension ie too much an it will want to pivot like a swingarm lol I know what I mean though my description is poor!!!

replies.author: @steveharrison7615

author: @garyhardman8369
text:
I think that Luke should have his own YouTube channel.
Maybe some people like his presenting style and content. Personally I do not.
I think that the channels are diverse enough to be separate entities.

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: I agree, maybe FWG-2 ??

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I am sorry mate - I really am - but that is an opinion to be ashamed of. To want to brush him off to some corner of the net where he doesn't bother you or impinge on you for a few minutes of your time is really quite poor in my opinion. He is young and learning - here on my channel he gets exposure, experience and a chance to learn - do you think he would have the confidence to do what he is doing without help? Is it not our responsibility to the young to help them, teach them bring them on? not shove them away, seperate them and isolate them? You might have guessed by now I feel strongly about this and I woudl suggest if you don't like Luke's vid - don't watch them - certainly don't complain to me about them

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Steve!!! - I am shocked at you and feel great sorrow that you would agree with this - I do understand why at face value you would agree - but you must know there is more going on that would seem at face value - Rob

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering please read email, I am sorry I didn't read the centre of this comment and am ashamed that I responded without reading more carefully.

replies.author: @garyhardman8369
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
Firstly, I apologize if my reply caused any hurt. It was not intended to do so.
I was however, surprised at the level of vitriol in your reply.
what I was trying to convey was the fact that people in general watch YouTube channels which have content which is specific to their interests.
What you have shown is a common, but misguided parental trait where you feel the need to attack anybody, who dares to criticize their offspring in any and all circumstances.
To state that I "should be ashamed" for stating a fact,  I take as a personal insult.
For this reason, you will be glad to hear, I will no longer be a subscriber.

author: @azlandpilotcar4450

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @minutemanhomestead7214
text: looking great but please a small request don't hold the stick when you weld no matter how tempting it is to stabilize it it only takes a small hole in your glove and your in the hospital carry on good sir

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: Rubber gloves under the welding gloves would be a good idea. Might be inconvenient but safety is better than no more Rob for us to know.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: mate! - don't be that guy. Don't peddle fear under the guise of concern. If I burn a finger - so what? If it's the electricity you are saying you are worried about - then read - this machine outputs 20 volts i could grab that with my bare hands and nothing would happen

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: It's a 20 volt output machine mate - if i grabbed it with my bare hands nothing would happen - you wear gloves to protect you from the heat not the volts - rubber gloves under would do nothing at all but make you sweat.

replies.author: @minutemanhomestead7214
replies.text: it was literally the first thing i was told as a certified welder idk what the other guy was talkin about rubber gloves its more about slipping your hand down and grabbing the end of that stinger thats not a burn you want to have

author: @jamescunliffe9872
text: I'd add triangulation from the top of the head tube to the 90° at the front of the frame:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i went for 25 degrees from the perpendicular - mostly because that was the angle of the machine i took the forks from

replies.author: @jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering is that the rake/castor angle that I think is fine,just ment additional fixing on the head tube to bottom of the frame,talking of bottoms did I imagine in the comments a bear behind?!?!lol

replies.author: @jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I heard someone mention an off ninety degree head angle but that's only applicable to tiny polyurethane wheels that would skid out with too much castor,judging from the hub motor wheel the angle should be spot on,could even fit a seat capable of carrying two:)

author: @Thrive-Off-Grid
text: Remember the video you just did on simplicity?? Um yeah leave it electric, charge it with the gasifier / generator. You are still being transported by the biomass with less complexity, less weight, way less cumbersome and it will be more efficient.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you may have missed the point of the video - simplicty is clarity not lack of experimentation. Building a car to run off a gasifier is simpler anyway as you reduce the need to have two machines doing one hob - it hardly matters really anyway - i do what i do for my reasons and as long as i am clear in my objectives it is kept simple

replies.author: @Thrive-Off-Grid
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Right on Robert. Just a suggestion.

author: @EnterTheRealm
text: The angle of the steering columb may cause you some issues riding. I would suggest just off of 90°. Would do it for a scoot. And if you need advise on an esc. Let me know. :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i went for 25 degrees off vertical as that was the angle of the machine i took it from - but aren't choppers a steeper rake?

replies.author: @EnterTheRealm
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering ah right if you plan on being sitting down it may be okay.

replies.author: @EnterTheRealm
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering if you imagine where the handles bars would be whilst you are pushing along it woukd be awkward. But if you plan on using 100 percent electric all the time and have a seat that would probably be okay.
